days.title: Arrival in Barcelona
days.subtitle: Gaudí's City Awaits

timeline.title: Airport Arrival & Ground Transport
timeline.detail: Arrive at Barcelona-El Prat Airport. Proceed to baggage claim and arrange transfer to Hotel Barcelona Sants. Journey takes approximately 30 minutes via taxi or shuttle service.

timeline.title: Hotel Check-in
timeline.detail: Arrive at Hotel Barcelona Sants and complete check-in process. Store luggage and refresh from travel. Get hotel information and local maps from concierge.

timeline.title: Sagrada Família Exterior Visit
timeline.detail: Visit Gaudí's iconic unfinished basilica and admire its extraordinary façade and architectural details from outside. Take photos and explore the surrounding plaza area.

timeline.title: Park Güell Viewpoint Walk
timeline.detail: Take metro to Park Güell and explore the stunning terraced park with panoramic city views. Walk through the colorful mosaic structures and gardens designed by Gaudí.

timeline.title: Lunch at Local Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy traditional Catalan cuisine at a local restaurant in the Gràcia neighborhood near Park Güell. Try tapas, pan con tomate, and local seafood specialties.

timeline.title: Casa Batlló Exterior & Casa Amatller
timeline.detail: Walk through the Eixample district and admire the façades of Casa Batlló and Casa Amatller. Explore the modernist architecture and iconic Passeig de Gràcia boulevard.

timeline.title: Eixample District Shopping & Exploration
timeline.detail: Stroll through the grid-patterned streets of Eixample district, browse boutique shops, galleries, and cafés. Admire the modernist architecture of residential buildings.

timeline.title: Dinner & Nightlife in Gothic Quarter
timeline.detail: Head to Barcelona's Gothic Quarter for dinner at a traditional tapas bar. Experience local wine and Catalan dishes in atmospheric medieval streets.

timeline.title: Evening Drinks & Nightlife
timeline.detail: Visit a rooftop bar or cocktail lounge in the Gothic Quarter or Eixample for drinks and city views. Experience Barcelona's vibrant nightlife scene with local craft cocktails.
timeline.mapQuery: Gothic Quarter rooftop bars, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: Take metro or taxi back to Hotel Barcelona Sants. Rest and prepare for Day 2 of Barcelona exploration.

localTips: Get T-Casual 10-ride transit card at airport
Book Sagrada Familia tickets online in advance

days.title: Gaudí & Modernisme
days.subtitle: Architectural Masterpieces

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a full buffet breakfast at Hotel Barcelona Sants before heading out to explore Gaudí's masterpieces.

timeline.title: Metro to Sagrada Familia
timeline.detail: Take Metro Line 2 or 5 from Sants station directly to Sagrada Familia station (approximately 15 minutes).
timeline.mapQuery: Sagrada Familia Metro Station, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Sagrada Familia Basilica
timeline.detail: Explore Gaudí's unfinished masterpiece with its intricate Gothic-inspired architecture, soaring columns, and stunning stained glass. Book tickets in advance to skip queues.

timeline.title: Lunch in Eixample District
timeline.detail: Enjoy traditional Catalan cuisine at a local restaurant near Sagrada Familia, such as a tapas bar or seafood restaurant.

timeline.title: Metro to Park Güell
timeline.detail: Take Metro Line 3 from Lesseps station and walk uphill to Park Güell (approximately 30 minutes total including walk).

timeline.title: Park Güell Terraces & Mosaics
timeline.detail: Wander through Gaudí's whimsical park with colorful mosaic decorations, panoramic city views, and unique architectural elements. Allow 2-3 hours to fully explore.

timeline.title: Metro to Casa Batlló
timeline.detail: Take Metro Line 3 back toward city center and transfer to reach Casa Batlló (approximately 25 minutes).
timeline.mapQuery: Casa Batlló Metro Stop, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Casa Batlló Façade & Interior
timeline.detail: Admire the colorful, organic wavy façade of Casa Batlló and explore its imaginative interior design with curved rooms and natural light elements.
timeline.mapQuery: Casa Batlló, Passeig de Gràcia, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Dinner & Nightlife in Passeig de Gràcia
timeline.detail: Experience Barcelona's vibrant nightlife with dinner at an upscale restaurant followed by cocktails at a nearby bar or club in the fashionable Passeig de Gràcia area.
timeline.mapQuery: Restaurants Passeig de Gràcia, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Metro Back to Hotel
timeline.detail: Take Metro Line 3 from Passeig de Gràcia back to Sants station and return to Hotel Barcelona Sants (approximately 20 minutes).

timeline.title: Rest & Relaxation
timeline.detail: Return to your hotel, freshen up, and rest after a full day exploring Barcelona's most iconic modernist architecture.

localTips: Visit Sagrada Familia early morning (08:00) to beat crowds
Park Güell: book timed entry weeks ahead
Lunch at local mercat food stall in La Ribera

days.title: Gothic Quarter & Waterfront
days.subtitle: Medieval Streets & Modern Marina

timeline.title: Breakfast at hotel
timeline.detail: Start your day with breakfast at Hotel Barcelona Sants before heading to the Gothic Quarter.

timeline.title: Transport to Gothic Quarter
timeline.detail: Take metro Line 2 from Sants Estació to Liceu station (15 minutes) to reach the heart of Barri Gòtic.

timeline.title: Barcelona Cathedral
timeline.detail: Explore the stunning Gothic Cathedral with its intricate architecture, cloisters, and panoramic rooftop views of the old city.
timeline.mapQuery: Barcelona Cathedral, Barri Gòtic, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Las Ramblas promenade stroll
timeline.detail: Walk down the famous tree-lined Las Ramblas, browse street performers, shops, and soak in the vibrant atmosphere of central Barcelona.

timeline.title: Lunch at traditional tapas bar
timeline.detail: Enjoy authentic Spanish tapas including jamón ibérico, patatas bravas, and local wines at a cozy bar in Barri Gòtic.

timeline.title: Gothic Quarter medieval exploration
timeline.detail: Wander through narrow medieval streets, hidden plazas, and historic buildings of Barcelona's oldest neighborhood with its ancient Roman walls.

timeline.title: Transport to Port Vell
timeline.detail: Walk 10 minutes from Gothic Quarter or take a short metro ride to reach the waterfront marina.

timeline.title: Port Vell marina sunset
timeline.detail: Relax at the modern marina watching sailboats and enjoying views of the Mediterranean Sea as the sun sets over the water.

timeline.title: Tapas crawl in Barri Gòtic
timeline.detail: Embark on a guided or self-guided tour of multiple historic tapas bars, sampling local wines, croquetas, seafood, and regional specialties.

timeline.title: Nightlife at Gothic Quarter bar
timeline.detail: End the evening at a lively cocktail bar or wine lounge in the Gothic Quarter, enjoying drinks and conversation with locals and fellow travelers.

timeline.title: Return to hotel
timeline.detail: Take metro Line 2 back to Sants Estació and walk to Hotel Barcelona Sants for a well-deserved rest.

localTips: Avoid Las Ramblas late night pickpockets
Best pintxos in El Xampanyet bar

days.title: Montjuïc & Views
days.subtitle: City Vistas & Cultural Treasures

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely breakfast at Hotel Barcelona Sants before heading out to Montjuïc.

timeline.title: Travel to Montjuïc
timeline.detail: Take Metro L2 from Sants Estació to Paral·lel, then cable car or funicular up to Montjuïc hill.

timeline.title: Montjuïc Castle (Castell de Montjuïc)
timeline.detail: Explore this impressive 17th-century fortress with panoramic views of Barcelona and the Mediterranean Sea from its bastions.

timeline.title: Fundació Joan Miró Museum
timeline.detail: Immerse yourself in the world's largest collection of works by Joan Miró, featuring his surrealist paintings, sculptures, and installations across multiple galleries.

timeline.title: Lunch at Montjuïc
timeline.detail: Enjoy Mediterranean cuisine and local specialties at a restaurant overlooking the city with terrace seating.

timeline.title: Olympic Stadium (Estadi Olímpic)
timeline.detail: Visit the architectural landmark from the 1992 Barcelona Olympics, exploring its modernist design and sports heritage.
timeline.mapQuery: Estadi Olímpic Lluís Companys, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Montjuïc Gardens & Views
timeline.detail: Stroll through the beautiful Jardins de Mossèn Costa i Llobera with cacti and succulents, capturing sunset views over Barcelona.
timeline.mapQuery: Jardins de Mossèn Costa i Llobera, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Dinner in Poble Espanyol or nearby
timeline.detail: Enjoy traditional Catalan and Spanish cuisine at a local restaurant before the fountain show.

timeline.title: Magic Fountain Evening Show
timeline.detail: Experience the spectacular Font Màgica light, music, and water fountain show illuminating Montjuïc's Plaza Espanya with synchronized choreography.
timeline.mapQuery: Font Màgica, Plaza Espanya, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Nightlife
timeline.detail: Head back to Hotel Barcelona Sants via metro, or explore nearby nightlife venues in the Sants district for evening drinks and cocktails.

localTips: Cable car up Montjuïc for 2.50€ views
Picnic supplies from Mercat de la Boqueria

days.title: Departure & Last Bites
days.subtitle: Farewell to Barcelona

timeline.title: Hotel Checkout
timeline.detail: Check out from Hotel Barcelona Sants. Store luggage if needed for later departure.

timeline.title: Taxi to La Boqueria Market
timeline.detail: Take taxi from hotel to La Boqueria Market for final morning visit. Journey takes approximately 15-20 minutes.

timeline.title: La Boqueria Market Final Visit
timeline.detail: Explore the vibrant market one last time, sample fresh juice, browse local produce and delicacies for souvenirs or last-minute tastings.
timeline.mapQuery: La Boqueria Market, Las Ramblas, Barcelona, Spain

timeline.title: Taxi to Barceloneta Beach
timeline.detail: Travel to Barceloneta neighborhood near the beach for seaside brunch experience. Journey takes about 15 minutes.

timeline.title: Seaside Chiringuito Brunch
timeline.detail: Enjoy a relaxed beachfront brunch at a traditional chiringuito (beach bar) with fresh seafood, paella, or local specialties while overlooking the Mediterranean.

timeline.title: Last-Minute Modernist Architecture Walk
timeline.detail: Stroll through Barceloneta and Gothic Quarter to discover hidden modernist details, facades, and architectural gems you may have missed.

timeline.title: Retrieve Luggage & Light Snack
timeline.detail: Return to Hotel Barcelona Sants to collect stored luggage. Grab a quick coffee and pastry at a nearby café.

timeline.title: Taxi to Barcelona Airport
timeline.detail: Depart hotel for Barcelona-El Prat Airport. Journey takes approximately 20-30 minutes depending on traffic.

timeline.title: Airport Check-In & Security
timeline.detail: Arrive at airport, check in baggage, and proceed through security screening for departure flight.

timeline.title: Departure
timeline.detail: Board flight and depart Barcelona. Reflect on memories of architecture, culture, and culinary discoveries throughout the trip.

localTips: Buy jamón ibérico as souvenirs at airport

hotels.name: Hotel Barcelona Sants
hotels.note: 3-min walk from Sants station, Eixample location

budget.note: Malaysia Airlines or similar, economy return

urgent.label: Book Sagrada Familia tickets online
urgent.note: Sells out 2-4 weeks in advance, skip-the-line crucial

urgent.label: Reserve Park Güell timed entry
urgent.note: Max 400 visitors per 30-min slot, book immediately

urgent.label: Book international flights KUL-BCN
urgent.note: 6+ weeks advance recommended for June peak season

urgent.label: Confirm hotel in Eixample/Sants
urgent.note: Summer season fills quickly, reserve now
